title: AIIMS Gorakhpur Junior Resident Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 12 Posts
organization: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Gorakhpur
short_info: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, Gorakhpur (AIIMS Gorakhpur) Junior Resident Notification 2026 has been released on 30 May 2026 on the official website of AIIMS Gorakhpur. A total of 12 vacancies have been announced through the AIIMS Gorakhpur Junior Resident Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 37 years Age Relaxation: SC/ST up to 5 years, OBC up to 3 years, PwBD up to 10 years (General), 13 years (OBC), 15 years (SC/ST)

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS from MCI recognized institute with completed compulsory rotatory internship
qualification.details: MBBS from MCI recognized institute. Candidate must have completed compulsory rotatory internship and must produce an internship completion certificate.

salary_details.details: In Pay Matrix (Level-10) with a minimum of Rs.56,100/- + NPA (for medical personnel) as per recommendations of the 7th CPC.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 12 vacancies announced for the Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) positions at AIIMS Gorakhpur.

faq.question: Q2. What is the eligibility criteria for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess an MBBS degree from an MCI recognized institute and have completed their compulsory rotatory internship. An internship completion certificate is mandatory.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for this post is 37 years. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories as per government norms.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) post?
faq.answer: The selected candidates will receive a salary in Pay Matrix (Level-10) with a minimum of Rs. 56,100/- per month, plus NPA for medical personnel as per the 7th CPC recommendations.

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) position?
faq.answer: The application process is through a Walk-in Interview. Candidates need to report at the specified venue on the interview date with all required documents.

faq.question: Q6. What is the date of the Walk-in Interview?
faq.answer: The Walk-in Interview for the Non-Academic Junior Resident (Medical) posts is scheduled for 08 June 2026.

faq.question: Q7. What documents should I carry for the Walk-in Interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, one set of self-attested photocopies, ID proof, educational certificates, internship completion certificate, and category certificate (if applicable).

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for the recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 500 for General/OBC candidates and Rs. 250 for SC/ST candidates. PwBD candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q9. Where is the venue for the Walk-in Interview?
faq.answer: The official notification contains the detailed venue address. Candidates are advised to check the notification for the exact location.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent or ad-hoc position?
faq.answer: This is an Ad-hoc job type position.
